note: The hero: a hardware panel built from the component referents. Positions are millimetres from frame centre, +y up (Blender). Only components with a referent on both sides appear here. Component sizes are not specified — every one is fixed by --ambx-grid (4px = 4mm) on the CSS side and by the matching GRID multiples in ambient3d/referents.py.

css.note: DOM-only theme. The referents are built from neutral calibration materials (referents.py: albedo 0.82 surfaces, 0.06 accents), so the CSS side drops the shipped hue-234/15% tint and the coral highlight — otherwise the wipe would cross a colour boundary as well as a renderer boundary. #373737 is the accent measured off the rendered frame.

body.note: The device: a chamfered slab standing off the ground. This is the calibration plate (ambient3d/components/plate.py) — the most directly grounded object in the project — and its CSS peer is the plain `.ambient .amb-surface .amb-chamfer-2` box. Controls recess into its top face, so the panel reads as one machined faceplate rather than parts floating on an infinite ground.
